response:
Short answer: we tightened SyncEventGrid so it only syncs Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions and we filter out anything that looks like a customer-owned/non‑Rackspace subscription — but we still use the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential (no per‑customer tokens).

Details:
- SyncEventGrid no longer scopes simply by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks.  
- It now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions → lighthouse_delegations and requiring managed_by_tenant_id == Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in {active, succeeded}.  
- The sync continues to use the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per‑customer tenant tokens.  
- Before upsert, rows are run through a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must follow Rackspace naming (rax-/rackspace-) and the destination must match Rackspace‑managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance‑v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr‑fleet/compliance resource IDs).  
- Sync stats and GetEventGridDeployments queries were updated to reflect the managed monitoring scope (distinguish skipped/unmanaged items and hide out‑of‑scope inventory).
context_injected:
--- Episode 1 [codex 2026-04-08] (similarity: 0.6159) ---
On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upsert by a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must match Rackspace naming conventions (rax-/rackspace-) and destination must match Rackspace-managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs). Sync stats now distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing subscriptions from SubscriptionNotFound warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments. GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries now use the same managed monitoring scope so page KPIs reflect only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions and hide old out-of-scope inventory.

--- Episode 2 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.517) ---
On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding changed event_grid_result['skipped'] back to a boolean and added skipped_no_monitoring as the explicit count, with finalize_run updated to carry both fields. Targeted Event Grid tests passed and all new review threads were replied to and resolved.

--- Episode 3 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.5151) ---
For azr-compliance-v3 PR #72 on 2026-04-09, two live GitHub review threads on GetEventGridDeployments were valid and fixed in commit 633573dc on develop. Fixes: (1) summary query no longer prepends an extra WITH before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE, preventing invalid SQL 'WITH WITH ...'; (2) selected tenant_id now uses COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id) to match join/filter fallback behavior. Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, replied to both threads, and resolved them.

--- Episode 4 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.5125) ---
On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, Event Grid eligibility was tightened so Event Grid subscriptions are only created/maintained for monitoring-entitled service blocks. Changes were made locally in source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, and OpenAPI docs. Customer-wide Event Grid orchestration now uses only Lighthouse subscriptions with computed_service_tier='advanced'; ensure_event_subscription_single now skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring subscriptions; the targeted Event Grid API returns 409 for subscriptions without monitoring entitlement; onboarding foundation state now marks non-monitoring subscriptions as Event Grid skipped rather than failed. Validation passed with pytest on test_eventgrid_helpers.py and test_http_eventgrid.py plus pre-commit and validate-openapi-spec.

--- Episode 5 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.5046) ---
Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk once, polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges latest item statuses into visible deployable cards, and shows a Latest Bulk Remediation panel.

--- Episode 6 [codex 2026-03-23] (similarity: 0.5034) ---
Implemented Event Grid name reconciliation in fleet-platform-app on 2026-03-23. Canonical Event Grid subscription name remains rax-resource-change-{customer_id[:12]}. Added shared classification utilities for live subscription-scoped Event Grid resources in shared/eventgrid_utils.py, including extraction of resource name, webhook URL, delivery attributes, and classification into canonical / legacy Fleet-targeting / external. Hardened postgres operational reads to be deterministic with ORDER BY updated_at DESC, id DESC and added list_eventgrid_subscription_records(subscription_id). Updated ensure_event_subscription_single to list live Event Grid subscriptions, fail closed on ambiguous multiple legacy Fleet-targeting names, delete a single legacy Fleet-targeting name before canonical create, delete stale legacy names when canonical already exists, and prune stale eventgrid_subscriptions rows after reconciliation. Updated delete_event_subscription_single to delete both canonical and legacy Fleet-targeting Event Grid resources for a subscription while preserving unrelated external subscriptions. Updated http_eventgrid_subscription_get to keep event_subscription_name canonical while exposing actual_event_subscription_name, name_mismatch, and reconciliation_status from live Azure discovery, and to avoid backfilling a legacy name as canonical. Updated liveness/list responses to expose reconciliation fields from current registry state. Updated drift detection/remediation to treat legacy-only live resources as name_mismatch drift and reconcile by deleting the legacy actual resource and recreating canonical. OpenAPI source and APIM specs were updated for the new response fields. Targeted tests passed for eventgrid helpers, http_eventgrid, liveness, and remediation; pre-commit hooks passed.

--- Episode 7 [codex 2026-03-27] (similarity: 0.4787) ---
On 2026-03-27 in fleet-platform-app, investigated why global Event Grid filters were not excluding Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces in prod. Live Azure event subscription rax-resource-change-1193525 on subscription 025d123f-b6ac-470a-a420-bf4d3087670f had advanced filters on key data.resourceInfo.type, but a captured raw EventGridSchema ResourceWriteSuccess payload showed subject and data.resourceUri fields, not data.resourceInfo.type. This made the filter ineffective even though it was deployed. Patched functions/shared/eventgrid_filters.py to use Subject-based StringNotContains filters with resource-ID path fragments (for example /providers/Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces/), updated focused tests, and verified Event Grid helper/http/drift tests passed. Existing prod subscriptions will still need Event Grid update/remediation to pick up the corrected filters.

--- Episode 8 [codex 2026-04-09] (similarity: 0.4779) ---
On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.

--- Episode 9 [codex 2026-03-20] (similarity: 0.4758) ---
Implemented customer/subscription-specific Event Grid filter override support in fleet-platform-app on 2026-03-20. Added Postgres table eventgrid_filter_overrides via migration 034 and schema sync, PgStore methods resolve_eventgrid_filter_override and upsert_eventgrid_filter_override, global default filters for Event Grid subscriptions, override resolution precedence subscription > customer > global default in functions/activities/eventgrid.py, eventTypes parameter support in the ARM event subscription template, and token-rotation preservation of eventTypes/advancedFilters in functions/triggers/http_eventgrid.py. Added unit tests for Event Grid helper override application and PgStore override read/write behavior; pytest and pre-commit passed on touched files.

--- Episode 10 [codex 2026-03-27] (similarity: 0.4572) ---
On 2026-03-27 for azr-fleet-platform PR #72, three live GitHub review threads were fetched and all were valid. Fixes landed in commit 201178f on dev. First, clarified the comment in functions/shared/eventgrid_filters.py to state that subscription-scoped EventGridSchema/CloudEvents use top-level subject and optional data.resourceUri, not data.resourceInfo.type. Second, added runtime normalization in resolve_eventgrid_filter_profile() so legacy DB overrides using data.resourceInfo.type are converted to Subject-based StringNotContains filters with normalized path fragments, preserving existing override behavior after the default filter-key change. Third, hardened the helper test to locate the default filter by distinctive values instead of list position. Validated with ruff plus pytest on test_eventgrid_helpers.py, test_http_eventgrid.py, and test_eventgrid_remediate_drift.py; replied on all three PR threads and resolved them.
